step_by_step_reasoning: First, we translate each of the 11 entities listed in the World Model into a node, mapping their service, name, and type accurately. Next, we build the four explicit edges defined in the visual connections. The first workflow (flow_id 0) is Macie (Main) sending metadata findings to the central S3 findings bucket. The second workflow (flow_id 1) represents interactive querying, where Athena queries the S3 findings bucket and QuickSight visualizes this data. The third workflow (flow_id 2) covers the pipeline where data from the S3 findings bucket is moved/loaded into the custom MySQL data lake (facilitated by Glue, as referenced in the transcripts).

graph.name: Opportune Data Security Posture Management with Amazon Macie
graph.notes: Opportune uses Amazon Macie across multiple AWS accounts to perform automated sensitive data discovery (like SSNs and ITINs) on their S3 buckets. The findings (metadata) are aggregated into a central S3 bucket. They use Amazon Athena to query this data, Amazon QuickSight to visualize insights and share them with bucket owners, and AWS Glue to load the aggregated findings into a custom MySQL data lake for deeper reporting and remediation workflows.

nodes.name: Security Engineers
nodes.notes: Internal security engineering team managing the posture and remediation process.

nodes.name: Amazon Athena
nodes.notes: Queries S3 findings database using SQL for data discovery.

nodes.name: Amazon QuickSight
nodes.notes: Used to create dashboards for telling a better story of risky buckets and PII types.

nodes.name: S3 Bucket (Findings)
nodes.notes: The centralized green bucket where Macie outputs discovery findings.

nodes.name: MySQL
nodes.notes: A custom MySQL data lake used to house findings for analysis.

nodes.name: AWS Glue
nodes.notes: Used to run automated ETL workflows for parsing and loading findings data.

nodes.name: Amazon Macie (Main)
nodes.notes: The Macie instance running in the main/security account, collecting data.

nodes.name: Amazon Macie (Top Right)
nodes.notes: Macie running in a connected member AWS account.

nodes.name: Amazon Simple Storage Service (Top Right)
nodes.notes: Production S3 bucket scanned by Macie in a connected AWS account.

nodes.name: Amazon Macie (Bottom Right)
nodes.notes: Macie instance in another connected member AWS account.

nodes.name: Amazon Simple Storage Service (Bottom Right)
nodes.notes: Another production S3 bucket scanned by Macie.

edges.notes: Macie sends its metadata findings into the central findings S3 bucket.
Athena runs SQL queries against the findings stored in the S3 bucket.
QuickSight accesses findings data from the bucket (via Athena) to generate dashboards.
Data is extracted from the findings S3 bucket to be loaded into the MySQL data lake.
